description: Behaviour lives here in Git (single source of truth). The Mend app is only the runner — nothing app-specific lives at Mend (all images/charts/k0s are public, no registry creds).

kubernetes.description: Renovate's kubernetes manager has no default file scope — point it at our manifests so image tags (e.g. viren070/aiostreams) get bump PRs.

flux.description: Our HelmReleases + HelmRepositories live under infrastructure/ and observability/, not the flux manager's default flux/ dir — point it here so pinned chart versions (cilium, traefik, cert-manager, loki, kube-prometheus-stack…) get bump PRs. Config-only: nothing deploys until a PR is merged and Flux reconciles it.

customManagers.description: k0s host binary pinned in bootstrap.sh — PR on new releases; apply stays deliberate (Autopilot Plan, ADR 0015). Tags look like v1.35.2+k0s.0.

packageRules.description: k0s updates are NOT applied by Flux — the PR is a trigger, you apply the update deliberately (ADR 0015). Flag it so it never gets auto-merged.

cloudnative-pg/postgresql is NOT an independent dependency — it's a TOOL image (oidc-reconciler, restore-drill, chown init) that must mirror the CNPG database major (currently 17). The restore-drill boots a Postgres on data restored from the pg17 DB; pg18 binaries can't start a pg17 data dir, so a blind bump would break the backup drill. Renovate can't see the DB version (it's in a CNPG Cluster CRD it doesn't parse), so it must not drive this bump. The :17 tag already floats to the latest 17.x; bump every reference by hand, together, during a deliberate DB major migration.
